metadata:

# Diet Tracker

A personal diet tracking module for logging meals, monitoring calorie intake, and visualizing nutrition trends.

## Features

- **Meal Logging**: Record meals with food items, calorie counts, timestamps, and category tags (protein, carbs, fat, fiber)
- **Daily Goals**: Set a daily calorie target and track progress toward it
- **Daily Totals**: View total calories consumed per day with a breakdown by food category
- **Weekly Chart**: Bar chart showing daily calorie intake over the past 7 days with goal line overlay

## Usage

### Log a Meal
Add a meal entry with one or more food items, each tagged with a category and calorie count.

### Set Daily Goal
Configure your target daily calorie intake (e.g., 2000 kcal).

### View Today's Summary
See all meals logged today, total calories, category breakdown, and progress toward your daily goal.

### View Weekly Chart
A bar chart of the last 7 days of calorie intake, with your daily goal shown as a reference line.

## Data Storage
All meal data and goal settings are persisted in the `data/` directory as JSON files.

# Expense Logger

A personal expense tracking module for recording and analyzing spending.

## Features

- **Record expenses** with amount, category, date, and optional notes
- **Categories**: food, transport, housing, entertainment, utilities, other
- **Monthly summary** with category breakdowns rendered as a pie chart
- **Filter** expenses by date range and/or category
- **Export** filtered or full data as CSV

## Usage

### Adding an expense
Provide an amount, select a category, pick a date (defaults to today), and optionally add a note.

### Viewing summaries
Select a month to see total spending and a pie chart breaking down expenses by category.

### Filtering
Filter the expense list by start/end date and category to drill into specific spending patterns.

### Exporting
Export all or filtered expenses to CSV for use in spreadsheets or other tools.

---
name: inventory-manager
description: Track inventory items with low-stock alerts, barcode scanning, bulk import, and search
metadata:
  contentType: logistics
  structure: collection
  boundary: ask
  scale: "2"
---

# Inventory Manager

Manages a collection of inventory items with stock-level tracking and low-stock alerting.

## Features

- **Item tracking**: Each item has a name, SKU, quantity, category, and reorder threshold
- **Low-stock dashboard**: Items whose quantity drops below their reorder threshold are surfaced as alerts
- **Barcode input**: Text field with scanner integration placeholder — paste or scan a SKU to look up or add items
- **Bulk import**: Import items from a JSON array to populate or update inventory in batch
- **Search**: Find items by name or SKU with partial matching

## Data Model

Each inventory item:
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| id | string | Unique identifier (UUID) |
| name | string | Item display name |
| sku | string | Stock Keeping Unit code |
| quantity | number | Current stock count |
| category | string | Item category for grouping |
| reorderThreshold | number | Alert when quantity falls below this |
| createdAt | string | ISO timestamp of creation |
| updatedAt | string | ISO timestamp of last update |

## Usage

- **Add item**: Provide name, SKU, quantity, category, and reorder threshold
- **Update quantity**: Adjust stock levels for existing items
- **Search**: Enter a name or SKU fragment to filter the collection
- **Bulk import**: Supply a JSON array of item objects to import
- **Dashboard**: View all items with low-stock items highlighted at the top

- **Barcode input** — dashed-border scanner field that looks up items by SKU
- **Bulk import** — JSON textarea with validation, duplicate-SKU skipping, and error reporting

---
name: reading-list
description: Track books with reading progress, status management, and filtered views
metadata:
  contentType: education
  structure: list
  boundary: none
  scale: "2"
---

# Reading List

A personal reading tracker that lets you manage your book collection and monitor reading progress.

## Features

- **Add books** with title, author, genre, page count, and cover image URL
- **Track progress** by updating your current page number per book
- **Status management** — mark books as "want-to-read", "reading", or "completed"
- **Progress bar** shown per book based on current page vs total pages
- **Overall stats** — books completed, total pages read, average progress percentage
- **Filter** by reading status and/or genre

## Data Model

Each book record contains:
- `id` — unique identifier
- `title` — book title
- `author` — author name
- `genre` — genre category
- `pageCount` — total number of pages
- `currentPage` — current reading position (0 to pageCount)
- `coverUrl` — URL to cover image (optional)
- `status` — one of `want-to-read`, `reading`, or `completed`
- `dateAdded` — ISO timestamp when the book was added
- `dateCompleted` — ISO timestamp when marked completed (optional)

## Usage

- Add a new book by submitting the add-book form
- Update reading progress by setting the current page number
- Change book status via the status selector
- Use the filter controls to view books by status or genre
- View aggregate stats at the top of the list

export type ReadingStatus = 'want-to-read' | 'reading' | 'completed'

export type Book = {
  id: string
  title: string
  author: string
  genre: string
  pageCount: number
  currentPage: number
  coverUrl: string
  status: ReadingStatus
  dateAdded: string
  dateCompleted: string | null
}

export type ReadingStats = {
  totalBooks: number
  booksCompleted: number
  totalPagesRead: number
  averageProgress: number
}

export type FilterOptions = {
  status: ReadingStatus | 'all'
  genre: string
}

import type { Book, ReadingStats, FilterOptions } from './types'

  page: {

    fontWeight: '600',
    color: '#16213e',
    marginBottom: '4px',
  },
  bookAuthor: {
    fontSize: '14px',
    color: '#6c757d',
    marginBottom: '8px',
  },
  badge: {
    display: 'inline-block',
    padding: '2px 8px',
    borderRadius: '12px',
    fontSize: '12px',
    fontWeight: '500',
    marginRight: '6px',
  },
  progressContainer: {
    marginTop: '8px',
    width: '100%',
  },
  progressTrack: {
    width: '100%',
    height: '8px',
    backgroundColor: '#e9ecef',
    borderRadius: '4px',
    overflow: 'hidden',
  },
  progressLabel: {
    fontSize: '12px',
    color: '#6c757d',
    marginTop: '4px',
  },
  bookActions: {
    display: 'flex',
    gap: '8px',
    marginTop: '10px',
    flexWrap: 'wrap' as const,
    alignItems: 'center',
  },
  smallInput: {
    padding: '4px 8px',
    borderRadius: '4px',
    border: '1px solid #dee2e6',
    fontSize: '13px',
    width: '80px',
  },
  smallButton: {
    padding: '4px 10px',
    backgroundColor: '#0f3460',
    color: '#fff',
    border: 'none',
    borderRadius: '4px',
    fontSize: '12px',
    cursor: 'pointer',
  },
  emptyState: {
    textAlign: 'center' as const,
    padding: '48px 24px',
    color: '#6c757d',
    fontSize: '16px',
  },
}

const statusColors: Record<string, { bg: string; color: string }> = {
  'want-to-read': { bg: '#fff3cd', color: '#856404' },
  reading: { bg: '#cce5ff', color: '#004085' },
  completed: { bg: '#d4edda', color: '#155724' },
}

const statusLabels: Record<string, string> = {
  'want-to-read': 'Want to Read',
  reading: 'Reading',
  completed: 'Completed',
}

const styleStr = (obj: Record<string, string | number>): string =>
  Object.entries(obj)
    .map(([k, v]) => `${k.replace(/[A-Z]/g, (m) => `-${m.toLowerCase()}`)}:${typeof v === 'number' ? `${v}px` : v}`)
    .join(';')

const renderProgressBar = (current: number, total: number) => {
  const pct = total > 0 ? Math.round((current / total) * 100) : 0
  const barColor = pct === 100 ? '#28a745' : pct > 50 ? '#17a2b8' : '#0f3460'
  return h(
    'div',
    { style: styleStr(styles.progressContainer) },
    h(
      'div',
      { style: styleStr(styles.progressTrack) },
      h('div', {
        style: `width:${pct}%;height:100%;background-color:${barColor};border-radius:4px;transition:width 0.3s`,
      }),
    ),
    h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.progressLabel) }, `${current} / ${total} pages (${pct}%)`),
  )
}

const renderBookCard = (book: Book) => {
  const colors = statusColors[book.status] ?? statusColors['want-to-read']
  return h(
    'div',
    { style: styleStr(styles.bookCard) },
    book.coverUrl
      ? h('img', { src: book.coverUrl, alt: book.title, style: styleStr(styles.coverImg) })
      : h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.coverPlaceholder) }, h('span', {}, '\u{1F4D6}')),
    h(
      'div',
      { style: styleStr(styles.bookInfo) },
      h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.bookTitle) }, book.title),
      h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.bookAuthor) }, `by ${book.author}`),
      h(
        'div',
        { style: 'margin-bottom:8px' },
        h(
          'span',
          {
            style: `${styleStr(styles.badge)};background-color:${colors.bg};color:${colors.color}`,
          },
          statusLabels[book.status] ?? book.status,
        ),
        h(
          'span',
          { style: `${styleStr(styles.badge)};background-color:#e9ecef;color:#495057` },
          book.genre,
        ),
      ),
      renderProgressBar(book.currentPage, book.pageCount),
      h(
        'form',
        {
          method: 'POST',
          style: styleStr(styles.bookActions),
        },
        h('input', { type: 'hidden', name: 'bookId', value: book.id }),
        h('input', {
          type: 'number',
          name: 'currentPage',
          value: String(book.currentPage),
          min: '0',
          max: String(book.pageCount),
          style: styleStr(styles.smallInput),
          'aria-label': 'Current page',
        }),
        h('button', { type: 'submit', formaction: '/update-progress', style: styleStr(styles.smallButton) }, 'Update Page'),
        h(
          'select',
          { name: 'status', style: styleStr({ ...styles.select, padding: '4px 8px', fontSize: '13px' }) },
          h('option', { value: 'want-to-read', ...(book.status === 'want-to-read' ? { selected: '' } : {}) }, 'Want to Read'),
          h('option', { value: 'reading', ...(book.status === 'reading' ? { selected: '' } : {}) }, 'Reading'),
          h('option', { value: 'completed', ...(book.status === 'completed' ? { selected: '' } : {}) }, 'Completed'),
        ),
        h('button', { type: 'submit', formaction: '/update-status', style: styleStr(styles.smallButton) }, 'Set Status'),
        h('button', { type: 'submit', formaction: '/remove', style: styleStr(styles.buttonDanger) }, 'Remove'),
      ),
    ),
  )
}

const renderStatsBar = (stats: ReadingStats) =>
  h(
    'div',
    { style: styleStr(styles.statsBar) },
    h(
      'div',
      { style: styleStr(styles.statCard) },
      h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.statValue) }, String(stats.totalBooks)),
      h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.statLabel) }, 'Total Books'),
    ),
    h(
      'div',
      { style: styleStr(styles.statCard) },
      h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.statValue) }, String(stats.booksCompleted)),
      h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.statLabel) }, 'Completed'),
    ),
    h(
      'div',
      { style: styleStr(styles.statCard) },
      h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.statValue) }, stats.totalPagesRead.toLocaleString()),
      h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.statLabel) }, 'Pages Read'),
    ),
    h(
      'div',
      { style: styleStr(styles.statCard) },
      h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.statValue) }, `${stats.averageProgress}%`),
      h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.statLabel) }, 'Avg Progress'),
    ),
  )

const renderFilterBar = (genres: string[], filters: FilterOptions) =>
  h(
    'form',
    { method: 'GET', action: '/', style: styleStr(styles.filterBar) },
    h('label', { style: styleStr(styles.label) }, 'Status:'),
    h(
      'select',
      { name: 'status', style: styleStr(styles.select) },
      h('option', { value: 'all', ...(filters.status === 'all' ? { selected: '' } : {}) }, 'All'),
      h('option', { value: 'want-to-read', ...(filters.status === 'want-to-read' ? { selected: '' } : {}) }, 'Want to Read'),
      h('option', { value: 'reading', ...(filters.status === 'reading' ? { selected: '' } : {}) }, 'Reading'),
      h('option', { value: 'completed', ...(filters.status === 'completed' ? { selected: '' } : {}) }, 'Completed'),
    ),
    h('label', { style: styleStr(styles.label) }, 'Genre:'),
    h(
      'select',
      { name: 'genre', style: styleStr(styles.select) },
      h('option', { value: 'all', ...(filters.genre === 'all' ? { selected: '' } : {}) }, 'All Genres'),
      ...genres.map((g) => h('option', { value: g, ...(filters.genre === g ? { selected: '' } : {}) }, g)),
    ),
    h('button', { type: 'submit', style: styleStr(styles.button) }, 'Filter'),
  )

const renderAddForm = () =>
  h(
    'form',
    { method: 'POST', action: '/add', style: styleStr(styles.form) },
    h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.formTitle) }, 'Add a Book'),
    h(
      'div',
      { style: styleStr(styles.formGrid) },
      h(
        'div',
        {},
        h('label', { style: styleStr(styles.label), for: 'title' }, 'Title *'),
        h('input', { type: 'text', name: 'title', id: 'title', required: '', style: styleStr(styles.input) }),
      ),
      h(
        'div',
        {},
        h('label', { style: styleStr(styles.label), for: 'author' }, 'Author *'),
        h('input', { type: 'text', name: 'author', id: 'author', required: '', style: styleStr(styles.input) }),
      ),
      h(
        'div',
        {},
        h('label', { style: styleStr(styles.label), for: 'genre' }, 'Genre *'),
        h('input', { type: 'text', name: 'genre', id: 'genre', required: '', style: styleStr(styles.input) }),
      ),
      h(
        'div',
        {},
        h('label', { style: styleStr(styles.label), for: 'pageCount' }, 'Page Count *'),
        h('input', {
          type: 'number',
          name: 'pageCount',
          id: 'pageCount',
          required: '',
          min: '1',
          style: styleStr(styles.input),
        }),
      ),
      h(
        'div',
        {},
        h('label', { style: styleStr(styles.label), for: 'coverUrl' }, 'Cover Image URL'),
        h('input', { type: 'url', name: 'coverUrl', id: 'coverUrl', style: styleStr(styles.input) }),
      ),
      h(
        'div',
        {},
        h('label', { style: styleStr(styles.label), for: 'status' }, 'Status'),
        h(
          'select',
          { name: 'status', id: 'status', style: styleStr(styles.select) },
          h('option', { value: 'want-to-read' }, 'Want to Read'),
          h('option', { value: 'reading' }, 'Reading'),
          h('option', { value: 'completed' }, 'Completed'),
        ),
      ),
    ),
    h('button', { type: 'submit', style: styleStr(styles.button) }, 'Add Book'),
  )

export const renderPage = (
  books: Book[],
  stats: ReadingStats,
  genres: string[],
  filters: FilterOptions,
) => {
  return h(
    'html',
    { lang: 'en' },
    h(
      'head',
      {},
      h('meta', { charset: 'UTF-8' }),
      h('meta', { name: 'viewport', content: 'width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0' }),
      h('title', {}, 'Reading List'),
      h('style', {}, 'body{margin:0}*{box-sizing:border-box}'),
    ),
    h(
      'body',
      {},
      h(
        'div',
        { style: styleStr(styles.page) },
        h('h1', { style: styleStr(styles.header) }, 'My Reading List'),
        renderStatsBar(stats),
        renderFilterBar(genres, filters),
        renderAddForm(),
        books.length === 0
          ? h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.emptyState) }, 'No books yet. Add one above!')
          : h(Fragment, {}, ...books.map(renderBookCard)),
      ),
    ),
  )
}

---
name: bluesky-client
description: Bluesky social client for timeline feeds, posting, profiles, and follow management
metadata:
  contentType: social
  structure: feed
  boundary: ask
  scale: "2"
---

# Bluesky Client

A social client module for the Bluesky / AT Protocol network. Allows users to authenticate, browse their timeline, create posts, view profiles, and manage follows.

## Features

- **Authentication** — Log in with Bluesky handle and app password. Sessions are persisted locally.
- **Timeline Feed** — View the authenticated user's home timeline with author info, timestamps, and engagement counts.
- **Create Posts** — Compose and publish new text posts (up to 300 characters).
- **Profiles** — View any user's profile including display name, bio, follower/following counts, and recent posts.
- **Follow/Unfollow** — Follow or unfollow users from their profile view.
- **Like/Unlike** — Like or unlike posts directly from the feed.

## Usage

1. Call `login(identifier, password)` with your Bluesky handle (e.g. `user.bsky.social`) and an app password.
2. Use `getTimeline()` to fetch your home feed.
3. Use `createPost(text)` to publish a new post.
4. Use `getProfile(handle)` to view a user's profile.
5. Use `follow(did)` / `unfollow(followUri)` to manage follows.
6. Use `likePost(uri, cid)` / `unlikePost(likeUri)` to like or unlike posts.

## Rendering

All views are rendered as server-side HTML using the plaited template system. Each view returns an HTML string suitable for display.

export type SessionData = {
  did: string
  handle: string
  accessJwt: string
  refreshJwt: string
}

export type PostAuthor = {
  did: string
  handle: string
  displayName: string
  avatar: string | undefined
}

export type PostData = {
  uri: string
  cid: string
  author: PostAuthor
  text: string
  createdAt: string
  likeCount: number
  repostCount: number
  replyCount: number
  isLiked: boolean
  likeUri: string | undefined
}

export type ProfileData = {
  did: string
  handle: string
  displayName: string
  description: string
  avatar: string | undefined
  banner: string | undefined
  followersCount: number
  followsCount: number
  postsCount: number
  isFollowing: boolean
  followUri: string | undefined
}

export type TimelineResponse = {
  posts: PostData[]
  cursor: string | undefined
}

export type AuthResult = {
  success: boolean
  error?: string
  session?: SessionData
}

export type ActionResult = {
  success: boolean
  uri?: string
  error?: string
}

export { login, resumeSession, clearSession, isAuthenticated, getAgent } from './client'
export { getTimeline, getAuthorFeed } from './feed'
export { createPost, likePost, unlikePost } from './posts'
export { getProfile, follow, unfollow } from './profiles'
export { renderTimeline, renderProfile, renderCompose, renderLogin } from './render'
export type {
  SessionData,
  PostAuthor,
  PostData,
  ProfileData,
  TimelineResponse,
  AuthResult,
  ActionResult,
} from './types'

---
name: chat
description: Multi-room chat with message history, unread indicators, and text formatting
metadata:
  contentType: social
  structure: stream
  boundary: ask
  scale: "2"
---

# Chat Module

A real-time-style chat module supporting multiple rooms, persistent message history, and basic text formatting.

## Features

- **Room Management**: Create named chat rooms and browse available rooms to join.
- **Message History**: Each room stores messages locally as JSONL. Messages display sender name, text, and timestamp.
- **Unread Indicators**: Tracks the last-read message per room per user; rooms with new messages show an unread badge with count.
- **Typing Indicator**: Placeholder slot for showing when another user is typing.
- **Text Formatting**: Supports `**bold**` and `*italic*` markdown-style syntax in message bodies.

## Usage

1. **List rooms** — call `listRooms(userId)` to get all rooms with unread counts.
2. **Create a room** — call `createRoom(name)` to add a new chat room.
3. **Send a message** — call `sendMessage(roomId, sender, text)`.
4. **Get messages** — call `getMessages(roomId)` to retrieve full history.
5. **Mark read** — call `markRead(roomId, userId)` to clear unread count.
6. **Render UI** — call `renderRoom(roomId, userId)` or `renderRoomList(userId)` for HTML output.

---
name: discussion-forum
description: A threaded discussion forum with voting, nested replies, and sorting
metadata:
  contentType: social
  structure: thread
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Discussion Forum

A public discussion forum supporting threaded conversations with voting.

## Features

- **Create threads** with a title and body text, attributed to an author
- **Reply to threads** with nested replies (one level deep)
- **Vote** on threads and replies (upvote/downvote) to surface quality content
- **Sort threads** by newest, most upvoted, or most active (most replies)
- **Search** threads by title using a search bar

## Data Model

- **Thread**: id, title, body, author, createdAt, votes, replies
- **Reply**: id, body, author, createdAt, votes, parentThreadId

## Usage

All data is persisted to `data/threads.json`. The module exposes route handlers for creating, listing, voting, replying, and searching threads, plus server-side rendered views for the forum UI.

---
name: weather-dashboard
description: Display current weather conditions and 5-day forecast with temperature trend chart for any city
metadata:
  contentType: weather
  structure: object
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Weather Dashboard

Displays current weather conditions and a 5-day forecast for a given city.

## Features

- **Current conditions**: temperature (°C/°F), humidity, wind speed, weather description, and icon
- **5-day forecast**: daily high/low temperatures, weather description, and icons
- **Temperature trend chart**: simple line chart showing temperature changes across the forecast period
- **City search**: accept a city name to fetch weather data

## Usage

1. Enter a city name in the search input
2. View current weather conditions in the main panel
3. Scroll down to see the 5-day forecast cards
4. Review the temperature trend line chart at the bottom

## Data Format

Uses data structures compatible with the OpenWeatherMap API format. Placeholder data is provided for offline use; swap in real API calls by setting the `OPENWEATHERMAP_API_KEY` environment variable.

import { h } from 'plaited'
import type { CurrentWeather, DayForecast } from './types'
import { getWeatherIcon, formatTemp, celsiusToFahrenheit } from './weather'
import { renderTempChart } from './chart'

const styles = {
  container: 'max-width:700px;margin:0 auto;font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,sans-serif;color:#e0e0e0;background:#0f0f23;padding:24px;border-radius:12px;',
  header: 'text-align:center;margin-bottom:20px;',
  title: 'font-size:24px;font-weight:700;color:#ffffff;margin:0 0 4px 0;',
  subtitle: 'font-size:14px;color:#8888aa;margin:0;',
  searchForm: 'display:flex;gap:8px;margin-bottom:24px;',
  searchInput: 'flex:1;padding:10px 14px;border:1px solid #333355;border-radius:8px;background:#1a1a2e;color:#e0e0e0;font-size:15px;outline:none;',
  searchBtn: 'padding:10px 20px;border:none;border-radius:8px;background:#4ecdc4;color:#0f0f23;font-weight:600;font-size:15px;cursor:pointer;',
  currentCard: 'background:#1a1a2e;border-radius:12px;padding:20px;margin-bottom:20px;',
  currentTop: 'display:flex;align-items:center;gap:16px;',
  iconLarge: 'font-size:56px;line-height:1;',
  tempLarge: 'font-size:36px;font-weight:700;color:#ffffff;',
  description: 'font-size:16px;color:#aaaacc;text-transform:capitalize;',
  statsRow: 'display:flex;gap:24px;margin-top:16px;',
  statItem: 'display:flex;flex-direction:column;',
  statLabel: 'font-size:12px;color:#666688;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.5px;',
  statValue: 'font-size:16px;font-weight:600;color:#ccccee;',
  sectionTitle: 'font-size:18px;font-weight:600;color:#ffffff;margin:24px 0 12px 0;',
  forecastGrid: 'display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(5,1fr);gap:8px;',
  forecastCard: 'background:#1a1a2e;border-radius:10px;padding:14px 10px;text-align:center;',
  forecastDay: 'font-size:13px;font-weight:600;color:#8888aa;margin-bottom:6px;',
  forecastIcon: 'font-size:28px;margin:4px 0;',
  forecastTemp: 'font-size:13px;color:#ccccee;',
  forecastHigh: 'color:#ff6b6b;font-weight:600;',
  forecastLow: 'color:#4ecdc4;',
  forecastDetail: 'font-size:11px;color:#666688;margin-top:4px;',
  chartSection: 'margin-top:24px;',
}

export const renderSearchForm = (currentCity: string) =>
  h('div', { style: styles.searchForm },
    h('input', {
      type: 'text',
      placeholder: 'Enter city name...',
      value: currentCity,
      style: styles.searchInput,
      name: 'city',
      'p-trigger': 'keydown:search',
    }),
    h('button', {
      style: styles.searchBtn,
      'p-trigger': 'click:search',
    }, 'Search'),
  )

export const renderCurrentWeather = (data: CurrentWeather) => {
  const weather = data.weather[0]
  const icon = getWeatherIcon(weather?.icon ?? '01d')

  return h('div', { style: styles.currentCard },
    h('div', { style: styles.currentTop },
      h('span', { style: styles.iconLarge }, icon),
      h('div', {},
        h('div', { style: styles.tempLarge }, formatTemp(data.main.temp)),
        h('div', { style: styles.description }, weather?.description ?? 'Unknown'),
        h('div', { style: `font-size:13px;color:#666688;margin-top:2px;` },
          `Feels like ${formatTemp(data.main.feels_like)}`
        ),
      ),
    ),
    h('div', { style: styles.statsRow },
      h('div', { style: styles.statItem },
        h('span', { style: styles.statLabel }, 'Humidity'),
        h('span', { style: styles.statValue }, `${data.main.humidity}%`),
      ),
      h('div', { style: styles.statItem },
        h('span', { style: styles.statLabel }, 'Wind'),
        h('span', { style: styles.statValue }, `${data.wind.speed} m/s`),
      ),
      h('div', { style: styles.statItem },
        h('span', { style: styles.statLabel }, 'Pressure'),
        h('span', { style: styles.statValue }, `${data.main.pressure} hPa`),
      ),
      h('div', { style: styles.statItem },
        h('span', { style: styles.statLabel }, 'Visibility'),
        h('span', { style: styles.statValue }, `${(data.visibility / 1000).toFixed(1)} km`),
      ),
    ),
  )
}

export const renderForecastCards = (days: DayForecast[]) =>
  h('div', { style: styles.forecastGrid },
    ...days.map((day) =>
      h('div', { style: styles.forecastCard },
        h('div', { style: styles.forecastDay }, day.dayName),
        h('div', { style: styles.forecastIcon }, getWeatherIcon(day.icon)),
        h('div', { style: styles.forecastTemp },
          h('span', { style: styles.forecastHigh }, `${Math.round(day.tempMax)}°`),
          ' / ',
          h('span', { style: styles.forecastLow }, `${Math.round(day.tempMin)}°`),
        ),
        h('div', { style: styles.forecastDetail }, `💧 ${day.humidity}%  💨 ${day.wind} m/s`),
      )
    ),
  )

export const renderDashboard = (city: string, current: CurrentWeather, days: DayForecast[]) =>
  h('div', { style: styles.container },
    h('div', { style: styles.header },
      h('h1', { style: styles.title }, `${current.name}, ${current.sys.country}`),
      h('p', { style: styles.subtitle },
        `Updated: ${new Date(current.dt * 1000).toLocaleString()}`
      ),
    ),
    renderSearchForm(city),
    renderCurrentWeather(current),
    h('h2', { style: styles.sectionTitle }, '5-Day Forecast'),
    renderForecastCards(days),
    h('div', { style: styles.chartSection },
      h('h2', { style: styles.sectionTitle }, 'Temperature Trends'),
      renderTempChart(days),
    ),
  )

---
name: chart-generator
description: Generate bar, line, and pie charts from JSON data with auto field detection
metadata:
  contentType: science
  structure: form
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Chart Generator

Generate data visualizations from JSON data input. Supports bar charts, line charts, and pie charts with full customization.

## Features

- Accept JSON array of objects as data input
- Auto-detect numeric vs categorical fields
- Generate bar, line, and pie charts as SVG
- Customize chart title, axis labels, and colors
- Download charts as SVG files
- JSON editor with syntax highlighting

## Usage

1. Enter or paste JSON data (array of objects with numeric and string fields)
2. Click "Analyze" to auto-detect field types
3. Select chart type (bar, line, or pie)
4. Configure title, axis labels, category field, and value field
5. Optionally customize colors
6. Click "Generate" to create the chart
7. Click "Download SVG" to save the chart

## Data Format

Input should be a JSON array of objects:

```json
[
  { "month": "Jan", "revenue": 4200 },
  { "month": "Feb", "revenue": 5100 },
  { "month": "Mar", "revenue": 3800 }
]
```

Numeric fields are used for values (y-axis/slice size), categorical fields for labels (x-axis/slice labels).

export type ChartType = 'bar' | 'line' | 'pie'

export type FieldType = 'numeric' | 'categorical'

export type FieldInfo = {
  name: string
  type: FieldType
  sample: string | number
}

export type ChartConfig = {
  chartType: ChartType
  title: string
  xAxisLabel: string
  yAxisLabel: string
  categoryField: string
  valueField: string
  colors: string[]
  width: number
  height: number
}

export type DataRow = Record<string, string | number>

export type AnalysisResult = {
  fields: FieldInfo[]
  numericFields: string[]
  categoricalFields: string[]
  rowCount: number
}

export const DEFAULT_COLORS = [
  '#4e79a7', '#f28e2b', '#e15759', '#76b7b2', '#59a14f',
  '#edc948', '#b07aa1', '#ff9da7', '#9c755f', '#bab0ac'
]

[
  { "month": "Jan", "revenue": 4200, "expenses": 3100 },
  { "month": "Feb", "revenue": 5100, "expenses": 3400 },
  { "month": "Mar", "revenue": 3800, "expenses": 2900 },
  { "month": "Apr", "revenue": 6200, "expenses": 4100 },
  { "month": "May", "revenue": 7500, "expenses": 4800 },
  { "month": "Jun", "revenue": 6800, "expenses": 4200 },
  { "month": "Jul", "revenue": 8100, "expenses": 5100 },
  { "month": "Aug", "revenue": 7200, "expenses": 4700 },
  { "month": "Sep", "revenue": 6500, "expenses": 4300 },
  { "month": "Oct", "revenue": 7800, "expenses": 5000 },
  { "month": "Nov", "revenue": 8500, "expenses": 5500 },
  { "month": "Dec", "revenue": 9200, "expenses": 5900 }
]

---
name: interactive-map
description: Interactive map with markers, circles, categories, search, and sidebar
metadata:
  contentType: geo
  structure: collection
  boundary: ask
  scale: "2"
---

# Interactive Map

An interactive map module powered by Leaflet.js and OpenStreetMap tiles. Users can explore a map view, add color-coded markers with popups, draw circles to highlight areas, and search for locations.

## Features

- **Map view** — Pan/zoom OpenStreetMap tiles via Leaflet.js
- **Markers** — Add markers with title, description, and category; click to see popup
- **Circles** — Draw colored circles to highlight geographic areas
- **Search** — Location search bar using Nominatim geocoding
- **Sidebar** — Lists all markers grouped by category; click to center map
- **Categories** — Color-coded pin categories for organizing markers
- **Persistence** — Markers and categories saved to JSON data files

## Usage

1. Open the map module to see the default map view
2. Use the search bar to find and navigate to a location
3. Click "Add Marker" to place a marker at the current map center
4. Assign categories to markers for color-coded organization
5. Draw circles to highlight regions of interest
6. Use the sidebar to browse and jump to any saved marker

  {
    "id": "marker-1",
    "title": "Central Park",
    "description": "A large public park in New York City",
    "lat": 40.7829,
    "lng": -73.9654,
    "category": "parks"
  },
  {
    "id": "marker-2",
    "title": "Golden Gate Bridge",
    "description": "Iconic suspension bridge in San Francisco",
    "lat": 37.8199,
    "lng": -122.4783,
    "category": "landmarks"
  }
]

metadata:

  structure: form

---

# Unit Converter

A comprehensive unit converter supporting five categories of measurement:

- **Length**: mm, cm, m, km, in, ft, yd, mi
- **Weight**: mg, g, kg, lb, oz
- **Temperature**: C, F, K
- **Volume**: ml, l, gal, qt, pt, cup, tbsp, tsp
- **Time**: ms, s, min, hr, day, week

## Usage

1. Select a category tab (Length, Weight, Temperature, Volume, Time)
2. Enter a numeric value in the input field
3. Choose the source unit ("from") and target unit ("to")
4. The converted result and the conversion formula are displayed automatically

## Features

- Category tabs for switching between unit types
- Displays both the converted value and the formula used
- Supports decimal precision for accurate conversions
- Temperature conversions use proper formulas (not simple ratios)

import { convert, getCategories, getUnitsForCategory, formatNumber } from './converter'
import { render, renderToString } from './ui'
import type { ConversionResult } from './converter'
import type { RenderParams } from './ui'

export {
  convert,
  getCategories,
  getUnitsForCategory,
  formatNumber,
  render,
  renderToString,
}

export type { ConversionResult, RenderParams }

---
name: calendar
description: Monthly calendar with event scheduling, day view, recurrence, and category color-coding
metadata:
  contentType: tools
  structure: steps
  boundary: none
  scale: "3"
---

# Calendar Module

A personal scheduling and calendar module that displays a monthly calendar grid with event indicators and supports full event lifecycle management.

## Views

- **Month View**: Displays a monthly grid with day cells. Days containing events show colored dots corresponding to event categories. Navigate between months with previous/next controls.
- **Day View**: Shows all events for a selected date in chronological order. Each event displays its title, time range, category badge, and description.
- **Event Form**: Create or edit events with title, date, start time, end time, description, category, and optional recurrence pattern.

## Event Categories

Events are color-coded by category:
- **Work** — blue
- **Personal** — green
- **Health** — red
- **Social** — orange

## Recurrence

Events support optional recurrence patterns:
- **Daily** — repeats every day
- **Weekly** — repeats on the same weekday
- **Monthly** — repeats on the same day of month

## Usage

1. Open the month view to see the current month's calendar grid
2. Click a day to see that day's events in the day view
3. Use the "Add Event" action to create a new event
4. Click an event to edit or delete it
5. Navigate between months using previous/next controls

metadata:
  contentType: art

---

# Color Palette Generator

Generates harmonious color palettes from a base color using established color theory rules.

## Harmony Modes

- **Complementary** — Two colors opposite on the color wheel (180deg apart)
- **Analogous** — Three colors adjacent on the wheel (30deg apart)
- **Triadic** — Three colors evenly spaced (120deg apart)
- **Split-Complementary** — Base + two colors adjacent to its complement (150deg/210deg)
- **Tetradic** — Four colors forming a rectangle on the wheel (90deg intervals)

## Features

- Accept base color via hex input or color picker
- Display swatches with hex, RGB, and HSL values
- Copy any color value to clipboard
- WCAG AA/AAA contrast checker between any two palette colors

## Usage

1. Enter a base hex color or use the color picker
2. Select a harmony mode
3. View generated swatches with all color formats
4. Click any color value to copy it
5. Select two colors to check WCAG contrast compliance

---
name: portfolio-gallery
description: Responsive portfolio gallery with project cards, filtering, search, sorting, and lightbox detail views
metadata:
  contentType: art
  structure: collection
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Portfolio Gallery

A module for displaying project portfolios in a responsive card grid.

## Features

- **Grid View**: Responsive card grid showing thumbnail, title, description, tech tags, and links
- **Filtering**: Filter projects by technology tag
- **Search**: Search projects by title
- **Sorting**: Sort by date added (newest/oldest) or alphabetically (A-Z/Z-A)
- **Detail View**: Full project page with:
  - Complete project description
  - Lightbox image carousel with navigation
  - Links to live demo and source code

## Data Format

Projects are stored in `data/projects.json`. Each project has:

| Field         | Type       | Description                        |
|---------------|------------|------------------------------------|
| id            | string     | Unique identifier                  |
| title         | string     | Project name                       |
| description   | string     | Short description for card view    |
| fullDescription | string   | Detailed description for detail view |
| thumbnail     | string     | URL for card thumbnail image       |
| images        | string[]   | URLs for lightbox carousel         |
| tags          | string[]   | Technology/category tags           |
| demoUrl       | string     | Link to live demo (optional)       |
| sourceUrl     | string     | Link to source code (optional)     |
| dateAdded     | string     | ISO date string for sorting        |

## Usage

- `GET /` — Grid view with optional query params: `?tag=`, `?q=`, `?sort=`
- `GET /project/:id` — Detail view for a single project
- `POST /projects` — Add a new project
- `PUT /projects/:id` — Update a project
- `DELETE /projects/:id` — Remove a project

---
name: drawing-canvas
description: Freehand drawing canvas with shapes, layers, and PNG export
metadata:
  contentType: art
  structure: form
  boundary: none
  scale: "3"
---

# Drawing Canvas

A browser-based drawing canvas module with full illustration support.

## Features

- **Freehand drawing** — smooth brush strokes with adjustable size
- **Shape tools** — rectangle, circle, and line with stroke color
- **Color picker** — full color selection for brush and shapes
- **Brush size** — adjustable width slider
- **Eraser** — erase portions of the current layer
- **Undo / Redo** — step backward and forward through drawing history
- **Clear canvas** — wipe the active layer or all layers
- **Save / Load** — export drawings as PNG, re-import saved PNGs
- **Layers panel** — add, remove, reorder layers with thumbnail previews and visibility toggles

## Usage

Start the server and open in a browser. Select a tool from the toolbar, pick a color and brush size, then draw on the canvas. Use the layers panel on the right to manage layers.

## Data

Saved drawings are stored in `data/drawings/` as PNG files with accompanying JSON metadata.

---
name: playlist-manager
description: Music playlist manager with playback controls, drag-to-reorder, shuffle, and repeat
metadata:
  contentType: entertainment
  structure: list
  boundary: none
  scale: "2"
---

# Playlist Manager

A personal music playlist manager that lets you organize your music library into playlists with full playback controls.

## Features

- **Create & manage playlists** — name, describe, and organize multiple playlists
- **Add songs** — track title, artist, album, duration, and album art URL
- **Drag-to-reorder** — reorder songs within a playlist via drag-and-drop
- **Playback controls** — play, pause, skip forward, skip back with a visual progress bar
- **Now Playing** — displays current song info with album art
- **Shuffle mode** — randomize playback order
- **Repeat mode** — loop the current playlist or single track

## Data

Playlists are stored as JSON in `data/playlists.json`. Each playlist contains an ordered array of songs.

## Usage

Use the module to create playlists, populate them with songs, and control playback through the rendered UI. The audio element is a placeholder since actual audio files are not available — the UI simulates playback state and progress.

  {
    "id": "default",
    "name": "My Favorites",

input: Create a Newton's laws simulator module. It should include three interactive simulations: (1) a simple pendulum showing oscillation with adjustable length and gravity, (2) a projectile motion simulator with adjustable angle and initial velocity showing trajectory path, and (3) a spring-mass system with adjustable spring constant and mass. Display real-time values for position, velocity, and acceleration. Include play/pause/reset controls and speed adjustment.
hint: Use RK4 numerical integration for pendulum and spring-mass (spring-mass oscillation: angular frequency ω = Math.sqrt(k/m), period T = 2π/ω). Analytical solution for projectile (x = v0*cos(a)*t, y = v0*sin(a)*t - 0.5*g*t^2). Render with server-side SVG using plaited h() templates. Client-side animation loop via requestAnimationFrame with physics inlined for 60fps. Store simulation parameters in data/presets.json. Separate concerns: types.ts (state/params/vectors), physics.ts (oscillation engines for pendulum, spring-mass), renderer.ts (SVG), simulation.ts (play/pause/reset/speed/tick), client.ts (animation loop + DOM events). MSS: contentType science, structure steps, mechanics track+chart, boundary all, scale 2.

trials.output:
The module is complete with 7 files:

- **package.json** — Module metadata with MSS tags (`science`, `steps`, `track`/`chart`, `all`, scale 2)
- **server.ts** — Bun HTTP server serving the HTML page and presets API
- **ui.ts** — Server-side HTML rendering using plaited `h()` with a dark-themed UI
- **simulation.ts** — Client-side JavaScript for all three simulations (exported as string, embedded inline)
- **physics.ts** — TypeScript types for pendulum, projectile, and spring state
- **data/presets.json** — Default simulation parameters
- **skills/physics-simulator/SKILL.md** — Skill definition with frontmatter

### Simulations

1. **Pendulum** — Swinging bob with trail visualization, angle arc display, gradient-shaded bob. Adjustable length (0.5-5m) and gravity (1-20 m/s²). Uses symplectic Euler: `omega += -(g/L)*sin(theta)*dt`, `theta += omega*dt`.

2. **Projectile** — Parabolic trajectory with auto-scaling grid, dashed theoretical path overlay, velocity vector arrow with arrowhead, trail dots. Adjustable angle (5-85°) and initial velocity (5-50 m/s). Launches on first play press.

3. **Spring-Mass** — Horizontal spring with zigzag visualization (color shifts with strain), mass block, force arrow, equilibrium marker, and a real-time position-vs-time graph below. Adjustable spring constant (1-50 N/m) and mass (0.1-5 kg).

### Controls
- Play/Pause/Reset buttons with active state styling
- Speed slider (0.1x - 3.0x)
- Real-time value cards showing position, velocity, and acceleration
- Tab navigation between the three simulations
- Canvas renders at device pixel ratio for crisp display on Retina screens

input.content:
---
name: physics-simulator
description: Interactive Newton's laws simulator with pendulum, projectile motion, and spring-mass systems
metadata:
  contentType: science
  structure: steps
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Physics Simulator

An interactive physics simulator demonstrating Newton's laws of motion through three classic systems.

## Simulations

### 1. Simple Pendulum
Simulates a pendulum oscillating under gravity. Adjustable parameters:
- **Length** (0.5 - 5.0 m): affects oscillation period
- **Gravity** (1.0 - 20.0 m/s²): affects oscillation speed

Displays angular position, tangential velocity, and acceleration in real time. Trail visualization shows the bob's path.

### 2. Projectile Motion
Simulates a launched projectile following a parabolic trajectory. Adjustable parameters:
- **Launch angle** (5° - 85°): direction of launch
- **Initial velocity** (5 - 50 m/s): launch speed

Shows theoretical trajectory overlay, actual path with trail dots, and velocity vector arrow. Displays x/y position, velocity components, and gravitational acceleration.

### 3. Spring-Mass System
Simulates a mass oscillating on a horizontal spring (Hooke's law). Adjustable parameters:
- **Spring constant k** (1 - 50 N/m): spring stiffness
- **Mass** (0.1 - 5.0 kg): attached mass

Visualizes spring compression/extension with color feedback, force arrow, and a position-vs-time graph. Displays displacement, velocity, and acceleration.

## Controls
- **Play**: starts or resumes the simulation (launches projectile on first press)
- **Pause**: freezes the simulation state
- **Reset**: returns simulation to initial conditions
- **Speed**: adjustable from 0.1x to 3.0x real-time

## Physics
All simulations use symplectic Euler integration with a fixed 4ms timestep for stable energy conservation.

trajectory.content:
The module is complete with 7 files:

- **package.json** — Module metadata with MSS tags (`science`, `steps`, `track`/`chart`, `all`, scale 2)
- **server.ts** — Bun HTTP server serving the HTML page and presets API
- **ui.ts** — Server-side HTML rendering using plaited `h()` with a dark-themed UI
- **simulation.ts** — Client-side JavaScript for all three simulations (exported as string, embedded inline)
- **physics.ts** — TypeScript types for pendulum, projectile, and spring state
- **data/presets.json** — Default simulation parameters
- **skills/physics-simulator/SKILL.md** — Skill definition with frontmatter

### Simulations

1. **Pendulum** — Swinging bob with trail visualization, angle arc display, gradient-shaded bob. Adjustable length (0.5-5m) and gravity (1-20 m/s²). Uses symplectic Euler: `omega += -(g/L)*sin(theta)*dt`, `theta += omega*dt`.

2. **Projectile** — Parabolic trajectory with auto-scaling grid, dashed theoretical path overlay, velocity vector arrow with arrowhead, trail dots. Adjustable angle (5-85°) and initial velocity (5-50 m/s). Launches on first play press.

3. **Spring-Mass** — Horizontal spring with zigzag visualization (color shifts with strain), mass block, force arrow, equilibrium marker, and a real-time position-vs-time graph below. Adjustable spring constant (1-50 N/m) and mass (0.1-5 kg).

### Controls
- Play/Pause/Reset buttons with active state styling
- Speed slider (0.1x - 3.0x)
- Real-time value cards showing position, velocity, and acceleration
- Tab navigation between the three simulations
- Canvas renders at device pixel ratio for crisp display on Retina screens

input.content:
export type Element = {
  atomicNumber: number
  symbol: string
  name: string
  atomicMass: number
  category: ElementCategory
  electronConfiguration: string
  oxidationStates: string
  meltingPoint: number | null
  boilingPoint: number | null
  discoveryYear: number | null
  description: string
  row: number
  col: number
}

export type ElementCategory =
  | 'alkali-metal'
  | 'alkaline-earth-metal'
  | 'transition-metal'
  | 'post-transition-metal'
  | 'metalloid'
  | 'nonmetal'
  | 'noble-gas'
  | 'lanthanide'
  | 'actinide'
  | 'unknown'

export type CategoryInfo = {
  label: string
  color: string
}

export const CATEGORY_COLORS: Record<ElementCategory, CategoryInfo> = {
  'alkali-metal': { label: 'Alkali Metal', color: '#ff6666' },
  'alkaline-earth-metal': { label: 'Alkaline Earth Metal', color: '#ffdead' },
  'transition-metal': { label: 'Transition Metal', color: '#ffc0cb' },
  'post-transition-metal': { label: 'Post-transition Metal', color: '#cccccc' },
  'metalloid': { label: 'Metalloid', color: '#cccc99' },
  'nonmetal': { label: 'Nonmetal', color: '#a0ffa0' },
  'noble-gas': { label: 'Noble Gas', color: '#c0ffff' },
  'lanthanide': { label: 'Lanthanide', color: '#ffbfff' },
  'actinide': { label: 'Actinide', color: '#ff99cc' },
  'unknown': { label: 'Unknown', color: '#e8e8e8' },
}

---
name: periodic-table
description: Interactive periodic table explorer with all 118 elements, category filtering, and detail views
metadata:
  contentType: science
  structure: collection
  boundary: all
  scale: "3"
---

# Periodic Table Explorer

An interactive periodic table module displaying all 118 elements in the standard 18-column layout.

## Features

- **Full periodic table** with all 118 elements in the standard layout including lanthanides and actinides
- **Color-coded categories**: alkali metals, alkaline earth metals, transition metals, post-transition metals, metalloids, nonmetals, noble gases, lanthanides, and actinides
- **Element cells** showing atomic number, symbol, name, and atomic mass
- **Detail panel** on click with electron configuration, oxidation states, melting/boiling points, discovery year, and description
- **Search bar** to find elements by name or symbol
- **Category filter** to highlight or isolate element groups

## Usage

Access the module to browse the periodic table. Click any element to view detailed information. Use the search bar to quickly find elements or the category filter to explore element groups.

Category assignments:
- H: nonmetal
- He, Ne, Ar, Kr, Xe, Rn, Og: noble-gas (Og is debated but commonly listed as noble gas or unknown — use noble-gas)
- Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr: alkali-metal
- Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra: alkaline-earth-metal
- B, Si, Ge, As, Sb, Te: metalloid
- C, N, O, P, S, Se, F, Cl, Br, I: nonmetal
- Al, Ga, In, Sn, Tl, Pb, Bi, Po, At: post-transition-metal
- Sc, Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, Y, Zr, Nb, Mo, Tc, Ru, Rh, Pd, Ag, Cd, Hf, Ta, W, Re, Os, Ir, Pt, Au, Hg, Rf, Db, Sg, Bh, Hs: transition-metal
- Mt, Ds, Rg, Cn: unknown (or transition-metal predicted — use unknown for these superheavy)
- Nh, Fl, Mc, Lv, Ts: unknown
- La, Ce, Pr, Nd, Pm, Sm, Eu, Gd, Tb, Dy, Ho, Er, Tm, Yb, Lu: lanthanide
- Ac, Th, Pa, U, Np, Pu, Am, Cm, Bk, Cf, Es, Fm, Md, No, Lr: actinide

The file should be a JSON array of 118 objects. Make sure all data is scientifically accurate. Use standard atomic masses (IUPAC 2021 values where possible). For electron configurations, use noble gas core notation (e.g., "[Xe] 6s1" for Cs).

IMPORTANT: Write ALL 118 elements. Do not skip any. The array must have exactly 118 objects.

---
name: statistics-calculator
description: Calculate descriptive statistics and render box plots and histograms for one or more named datasets
metadata:
  contentType: science
  structure: form
  boundary: all
  scale: "2"
---

# Statistics Calculator

Accepts datasets as comma-separated numbers or JSON arrays. Calculates descriptive statistics and renders visualizations for comparison.

## Statistics Computed

- **Central tendency**: mean, median, mode
- **Spread**: standard deviation, variance, range, IQR
- **Extremes**: min, max
- **Quartiles**: Q1 (25th percentile), Q3 (75th percentile)

## Input Formats

- Comma-separated: `1, 2, 3, 4, 5`
- JSON array: `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]`

## Features

- Add multiple named datasets for side-by-side comparison
- Summary table showing all statistics across datasets
- Box plot visualization per dataset
- Histogram visualization per dataset
- Save and load datasets from persistent storage

## Usage

1. Enter a dataset name and paste or type your numbers
2. Click "Add Dataset" to register it
3. View computed statistics, box plot, and histogram
4. Add more datasets to compare them in the summary table

---
name: recipe-manager
description: Save, search, and browse recipes with ingredients, steps, tags, and timing
metadata:
  contentType: produce
  structure: collection
  boundary: none
  scale: "2"
---

# Recipe Manager

A personal recipe collection manager. Users can create, view, search, and filter recipes.

## Features

- **Create recipes** with title, ingredients (amount, unit, name), numbered steps, tags, and prep/cook times
- **Browse recipes** in a searchable, filterable collection view
- **Filter by tags** such as vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, quick, etc.
- **Search by title** or ingredient name
- **View individual recipes** in a clean, readable layout
- **Edit and delete** existing recipes
- **Persistent storage** in JSON format

## Data Model

Each recipe contains:
- `id` — unique identifier
- `title` — recipe name
- `ingredients` — list of { amount, unit, name }
- `steps` — ordered list of instruction strings
- `tags` — array of dietary/category tags
- `prepTime` — preparation time in minutes
- `cookTime` — cooking time in minutes
- `createdAt` — ISO timestamp
- `updatedAt` — ISO timestamp

## Usage

- `GET /` — list all recipes with optional `?search=` and `?tag=` query params
- `GET /:id` — view a single recipe
- `POST /` — create a new recipe (JSON body)
- `PUT /:id` — update an existing recipe
- `DELETE /:id` — remove a recipe

```
├── store.ts              # CRUD operations with JSON file persistence
├── render.ts             # HTML rendering with plaited's h() and createSSR()
├── index.ts              # Bun.serve HTTP server with HTML + JSON API routes
├── data/recipes.json     # Persistent storage (starts empty)
└── skills/recipe-manager/SKILL.md
```

**Features:**

- **Create/Edit/Delete** recipes with title, ingredients (amount/unit/name), steps, tags, and prep/cook times
- **Searchable list** — search by recipe title or ingredient name
- **Tag filtering** — filter by tags like vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, etc. (15 common tags available as checkboxes)
- **Clean detail view** — shows timing badges, ingredient list, and numbered steps
- **Dual interface** — HTML forms for browser use, JSON API for programmatic access (`Accept: application/json`)
- **Persistent** — recipes stored in `data/recipes.json`

Run with `bun run index.ts` to start on port 3000.

---
name: habit-tracker
description: Track daily and weekly habits with streaks and completion rates
metadata:
  contentType: health
  structure: list
  boundary: none
  scale: "2"
---

# Habit Tracker

Track your daily habits and monitor weekly streaks.

## Features

- **Define habits** with a name and target frequency (daily or weekly)
- **Daily checklist** showing today's habits with toggle completion
- **Weekly streak view** showing which days each habit was completed
- **Completion rate** percentage for the current week
- **Persistent storage** of habit definitions and completion records

## Usage

### Add a habit
Provide a habit name and frequency (daily or weekly). Daily habits appear every day; weekly habits require completion at least once per week.

### Toggle completion
Mark habits as done or undone for today from the daily checklist.

### View streaks
The weekly view shows a 7-day grid (Mon–Sun) for each habit, highlighting completed days and calculating your overall completion rate.

### Delete a habit
Remove a habit and all its associated completion records.

   172→    .join(';')

   174→export const renderPage = (habits: Habit[], completions: Completion[]): string => {
   175→  const today = getToday()
   176→  const weekDates = getWeekDates()
   177→  const todayIdx = weekDates.findIndex((d) => d.date === today)
   178→
   179→  const weekViews: HabitWeekView[] = habits.map((habit) => {
   180→    const days = weekDates.map((wd, i) => ({
   181→      date: wd.date,
   182→      dayLabel: wd.dayLabel,
   183→      completed: isCompleted(completions, habit.id, wd.date),
   184→    }))
   185→
   186→    const expectedDays = habit.frequency === 'daily'
   187→      ? weekDates.filter((_, i) => i <= todayIdx || todayIdx === -1).length
   188→      : 1
   189→
   190→    const completedCount = days.filter((d) => d.completed).length
   191→
   192→    return { habit, days, completedCount, totalExpected: expectedDays }
   193→  })
   194→
   195→  // Overall completion rate
   196→  const totalCompleted = weekViews.reduce((sum, wv) => sum + wv.completedCount, 0)
   197→  const totalExpected = weekViews.reduce((sum, wv) => sum + wv.totalExpected, 0)
   198→  const completionRate = totalExpected > 0 ? Math.round((totalCompleted / totalExpected) * 100) : 0

   214→    },
   215→      h('option', { value: 'daily' }, 'Daily'),
   216→      h('option', { value: 'weekly' }, 'Weekly'),
   217→    ),
   218→    h('button', { type: 'submit', style: styleToString(styles.button) }, 'Add Habit'),
   219→  )
   220→
   221→  const checklist = habits.length === 0
   222→    ? h('div', { style: styleToString(styles.emptyState) }, 'No habits yet. Add one above to get started!')
   223→    : h('div', {},
   224→      ...habits.map((habit) => {

   229→            ...(done ? { checked: 'true' } : {}),

   232→            'data-habit-id': habit.id,
   233→            'data-date': today,

   237→          h('button', {
   238→            style: styleToString(styles.deleteButton),
   239→            'p-trigger': 'deleteHabit:click',
   240→            'data-habit-id': habit.id,
   241→          }, 'Delete'),
   242→        )
   243→      }),
   244→    )
   245→
   246→  const weekGridContent = habits.length === 0
   247→    ? h('div', { style: styleToString(styles.emptyState) }, 'Add habits to see your weekly streaks.')
   248→    : h(Fragment, {},
   249→      // Week grid
   250→      h('div', { style: styleToString(styles.weekGrid) },
   251→        // Header row
   252→        h('div', {}, ''),
   253→        ...weekDates.map((wd) =>
   254→          h('div', {
   255→            style: styleToString({
   256→              ...styles.dayHeader,
   257→              ...(wd.date === today ? { color: '#4f46e5', fontWeight: '700' } : {}),
   258→            }),
   259→          }, wd.dayLabel),
   260→        ),
   261→        h('div', { style: styleToString(styles.dayHeader) }, ''),
   262→        // Habit rows
   263→        ...weekViews.flatMap((wv) => [
   264→          h('div', {
   265→            style: styleToString({
   266→              fontSize: '13px',
   267→              fontWeight: '500',
   268→              overflow: 'hidden',
   269→              textOverflow: 'ellipsis',
   270→              whiteSpace: 'nowrap',
   271→            }),
   272→          }, wv.habit.name),
   273→          ...wv.days.map((day, i) => {
   274→            const isFuture = todayIdx >= 0 && i > todayIdx
   275→            const cellStyle = isFuture
   276→              ? { ...styles.dayCell, ...styles.dayCellFuture }
   277→              : day.completed
   278→                ? { ...styles.dayCell, ...styles.dayCellCompleted }
   279→                : { ...styles.dayCell, ...styles.dayCellMissed }
   280→
   281→            return h('div', { style: styleToString(cellStyle) },
   282→              day.completed ? '\u2713' : isFuture ? '\u00B7' : '\u2013',
   283→            )
   284→          }),
   285→          h('div', {
   286→            style: styleToString({
   287→              fontSize: '12px',
   288→              color: '#6b7280',
   289→              paddingLeft: '8px',
   290→            }),
   291→          }, `${wv.completedCount}/${wv.totalExpected}`),
   292→        ]),
   293→      ),
   294→      // Completion rate
   295→      h('div', { style: styleToString(styles.percentBar) },
   296→        h('div', { style: styleToString(styles.percentValue) }, `${completionRate}%`),
   297→        h('div', { style: styleToString(styles.percentLabel) }, 'Weekly completion rate'),
   298→      ),
   299→    )
   300→
   301→  const page = h('div', { style: styleToString(styles.container) },
   302→    h('div', { style: styleToString(styles.header) }, 'Habit Tracker'),

   314→      weekGridContent,
   315→    ),

   317→
   318→  return `<!DOCTYPE html>
   319→<html lang="en">
   320→<head>

   323→  <title>Habit Tracker</title>

   328→</head>
   329→<body>
   330→  ${page}
   331→</body>
   332→</html>`
   333→}
   334→
   335→const formatDate = (dateStr: string): string => {
   336→  const [year, month, day] = dateStr.split('-').map(Number)

   338→  return d.toLocaleDateString('en-US', {
   339→    weekday: 'long',
   340→    year: 'numeric',
   341→    month: 'long',
   342→    day: 'numeric',
   343→  })

input.content:
---
name: crypto-tracker
description: Track crypto portfolio holdings, values, gain/loss, and allocation
metadata:
  contentType: finance
  structure: list
  boundary: none
  scale: "2"
---

# Crypto Portfolio Tracker

Track your cryptocurrency portfolio with real-time gain/loss calculations and allocation visualization.

## Features

- **Add holdings** — Record coin symbol, amount held, and purchase price
- **Remove holdings** — Delete coins from your portfolio
- **Update prices** — Manually update current market prices for each coin
- **Portfolio overview** — View total portfolio value, per-coin gain/loss percentage, and allocation breakdown
- **Pie chart** — SVG allocation pie chart showing portfolio distribution by current value

## Usage

### Add a holding
Provide a coin symbol (e.g. BTC), the amount you hold, and the price you purchased at.

### Update a price
Set the current market price for any coin in your portfolio.

### View portfolio
See all holdings with current value, cost basis, gain/loss percentage, and an allocation pie chart.

## Data

Holdings and prices are persisted as JSON in the `data/` directory.

export type Holding = {
  id: string
  symbol: string
  amount: number
  purchasePrice: number
  addedAt: string
}

export type PriceMap = Record<string, number>

export type PortfolioEntry = {
  holding: Holding
  currentPrice: number
  currentValue: number
  costBasis: number
  gainLoss: number
  gainLossPercent: number
  allocationPercent: number
}

export type PortfolioSummary = {
  entries: PortfolioEntry[]
  totalValue: number
  totalCost: number
  totalGainLoss: number
  totalGainLossPercent: number
}

---
name: code-snippets
description: Save, browse, and filter code snippets by language and tag
metadata:
  contentType: tools
  structure: list
  boundary: none
  scale: "2"
---

# Code Snippets Manager

A personal code snippets manager for saving and organizing reusable code.

## Features

- **Save snippets** with a title, programming language, tags, and code content
- **Filter** snippets by programming language or tag
- **Copy to clipboard** with a single click
- **Syntax labelling** — each snippet displays its language prominently
- **Persistent storage** — snippets are saved to disk as JSON

## Usage

### Creating a snippet

POST to `/snippets` with a JSON body:

```json
{
  "title": "Array dedup",
  "language": "typescript",
  "tags": ["array", "utility"],
  "code": "const unique = <T>(arr: T[]): T[] => [...new Set(arr)];"
}
```

### Browsing snippets

GET `/` to see the full filterable list.

- `/?language=typescript` — filter by language
- `/?tag=utility` — filter by tag
- `/?language=python&tag=cli` — combine filters

### Deleting a snippet

DELETE `/snippets/:id` to remove a snippet.

export type Snippet = {
  id: string
  title: string
  language: string
  tags: string[]
  code: string
  createdAt: string
  updatedAt: string
}

import { h, Fragment } from '@plaited/template'
import type { Snippet } from './types'

const styles = {
  page: {
    fontFamily: '-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, sans-serif',
    maxWidth: '960px',
    margin: '0 auto',
    padding: '24px',
    backgroundColor: '#0d1117',
    color: '#c9d1d9',
    minHeight: '100vh',
  },
  header: {
    display: 'flex',
    justifyContent: 'space-between',
    alignItems: 'center',
    marginBottom: '24px',
    borderBottom: '1px solid #21262d',
    paddingBottom: '16px',
  },
  title: {
    fontSize: '24px',
    fontWeight: '700',
    color: '#f0f6fc',
    margin: '0',
  },
  filterBar: {
    display: 'flex',
    gap: '12px',
    marginBottom: '24px',
    flexWrap: 'wrap' as const,
  },
  select: {
    backgroundColor: '#161b22',
    color: '#c9d1d9',
    border: '1px solid #30363d',
    borderRadius: '6px',
    padding: '6px 12px',
    fontSize: '14px',
  },
  form: {
    backgroundColor: '#161b22',
    border: '1px solid #30363d',
    borderRadius: '8px',
    padding: '20px',
    marginBottom: '24px',
  },
  formTitle: {
    fontSize: '16px',
    fontWeight: '600',
    color: '#f0f6fc',
    margin: '0 0 16px 0',
  },
  fieldRow: {
    display: 'flex',
    gap: '12px',
    marginBottom: '12px',
    flexWrap: 'wrap' as const,
  },
  input: {
    backgroundColor: '#0d1117',
    color: '#c9d1d9',
    border: '1px solid #30363d',
    borderRadius: '6px',
    padding: '8px 12px',
    fontSize: '14px',
    flex: '1',
  },
  textarea: {
    backgroundColor: '#0d1117',
    color: '#c9d1d9',
    border: '1px solid #30363d',
    borderRadius: '6px',
    padding: '12px',
    fontSize: '13px',
    fontFamily: '"SFMono-Regular", Consolas, "Liberation Mono", Menlo, monospace',
    width: '100%',
    minHeight: '120px',
    resize: 'vertical' as const,
    boxSizing: 'border-box' as const,
  },
  button: {
    backgroundColor: '#238636',
    color: '#ffffff',
    border: 'none',
    borderRadius: '6px',
    padding: '8px 16px',
    fontSize: '14px',
    fontWeight: '600',
    cursor: 'pointer',
  },
  snippetCard: {
    backgroundColor: '#161b22',
    border: '1px solid #30363d',
    borderRadius: '8px',
    marginBottom: '16px',
    overflow: 'hidden',
  },
  snippetHeader: {
    display: 'flex',
    justifyContent: 'space-between',
    alignItems: 'center',
    padding: '12px 16px',
    borderBottom: '1px solid #30363d',
    backgroundColor: '#1c2128',
  },
  snippetTitle: {
    fontSize: '15px',
    fontWeight: '600',
    color: '#f0f6fc',
    margin: '0',
  },
  langBadge: {
    backgroundColor: '#388bfd26',
    color: '#58a6ff',
    padding: '2px 8px',
    borderRadius: '12px',
    fontSize: '12px',
    fontWeight: '500',
  },
  tagList: {
    display: 'flex',
    gap: '6px',
    padding: '8px 16px',
    flexWrap: 'wrap' as const,
  },
  tag: {
    backgroundColor: '#30363d',
    color: '#8b949e',
    padding: '2px 8px',
    borderRadius: '12px',
    fontSize: '12px',
  },
  codeBlock: {
    margin: '0',
    padding: '16px',
    overflow: 'auto',
    fontSize: '13px',
    lineHeight: '1.5',
    fontFamily: '"SFMono-Regular", Consolas, "Liberation Mono", Menlo, monospace',
    backgroundColor: '#0d1117',
    color: '#e6edf3',
    whiteSpace: 'pre',
  },
  actions: {
    display: 'flex',
    gap: '8px',
    alignItems: 'center',
  },
  copyBtn: {
    backgroundColor: '#21262d',
    color: '#c9d1d9',
    border: '1px solid #30363d',
    borderRadius: '6px',
    padding: '4px 10px',
    fontSize: '12px',
    cursor: 'pointer',
  },
  deleteBtn: {
    backgroundColor: '#21262d',
    color: '#f85149',
    border: '1px solid #30363d',
    borderRadius: '6px',
    padding: '4px 10px',
    fontSize: '12px',
    cursor: 'pointer',
  },
  empty: {
    textAlign: 'center' as const,
    padding: '48px 16px',
    color: '#8b949e',
    fontSize: '15px',
  },
}

const styleStr = (obj: Record<string, string | number>): string =>
  Object.entries(obj)
    .map(([k, v]) => `${k.replace(/[A-Z]/g, (c) => `-${c.toLowerCase()}`)}:${v}`)
    .join(';')

const renderSnippetCard = (snippet: Snippet) =>
  h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.snippetCard) },
    h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.snippetHeader) },
      h('span', { style: styleStr(styles.snippetTitle) }, snippet.title),
      h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.actions) },
        h('span', { style: styleStr(styles.langBadge) }, snippet.language),
        h('button', {
          style: styleStr(styles.copyBtn),
          'p-trigger': 'click:copy',
          'data-snippet-id': snippet.id,
          onclick: `navigator.clipboard.writeText(document.getElementById('code-${snippet.id}').textContent).then(()=>{this.textContent='Copied!';setTimeout(()=>this.textContent='Copy',1500)})`,
        }, 'Copy'),
        h('button', {
          style: styleStr(styles.deleteBtn),
          onclick: `fetch('/snippets/${snippet.id}',{method:'DELETE'}).then(()=>location.reload())`,
        }, 'Delete'),
      ),
    ),
    snippet.tags.length > 0
      ? h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.tagList) },
          ...snippet.tags.map((t) => h('span', { style: styleStr(styles.tag) }, t)),
        )
      : h(Fragment, {}),
    h('pre', { style: styleStr(styles.codeBlock), id: `code-${snippet.id}` },
      h('code', {}, snippet.code),
    ),
  )

export const renderPage = (
  snippets: Snippet[],
  languages: string[],
  tags: string[],
  currentLanguage?: string,
  currentTag?: string,
) => {
  const filterForm = h('form', { method: 'get', action: '/', style: styleStr(styles.filterBar) },
    h('select', { name: 'language', style: styleStr(styles.select), onchange: 'this.form.submit()' },
      h('option', { value: '' }, 'All Languages'),
      ...languages.map((lang) =>
        h('option', { value: lang, ...(lang === currentLanguage ? { selected: '' } : {}) }, lang),
      ),
    ),
    h('select', { name: 'tag', style: styleStr(styles.select), onchange: 'this.form.submit()' },
      h('option', { value: '' }, 'All Tags'),
      ...tags.map((t) =>
        h('option', { value: t, ...(t === currentTag ? { selected: '' } : {}) }, t),
      ),
    ),
  )

  const addForm = h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.form) },
    h('p', { style: styleStr(styles.formTitle) }, 'New Snippet'),
    h('form', { method: 'post', action: '/snippets' },
      h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.fieldRow) },
        h('input', { type: 'text', name: 'title', placeholder: 'Title', required: '', style: styleStr(styles.input) }),
        h('input', { type: 'text', name: 'language', placeholder: 'Language (e.g. typescript)', required: '', style: styleStr(styles.input) }),
      ),
      h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.fieldRow) },
        h('input', { type: 'text', name: 'tags', placeholder: 'Tags (comma-separated)', style: styleStr(styles.input) }),
      ),
      h('div', { style: styleStr({ marginBottom: '12px' }) },
        h('textarea', { name: 'code', placeholder: 'Paste code here...', required: '', style: styleStr(styles.textarea) }),
      ),
      h('button', { type: 'submit', style: styleStr(styles.button) }, 'Save Snippet'),
    ),
  )

  const snippetList = snippets.length > 0
    ? h(Fragment, {}, ...snippets.map(renderSnippetCard))
    : h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.empty) }, 'No snippets yet. Add one above!')

  return h('html', { lang: 'en' },
    h('head', {},
      h('meta', { charset: 'utf-8' }),
      h('meta', { name: 'viewport', content: 'width=device-width, initial-scale=1' }),
      h('title', {}, 'Code Snippets'),
      h('style', {}, 'body{margin:0;background:#0d1117}'),
    ),
    h('body', {},
      h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.page) },
        h('div', { style: styleStr(styles.header) },
          h('h1', { style: styleStr(styles.title) }, 'Code Snippets'),
        ),
        filterForm,
        addForm,
        snippetList,
      ),
    ),
  )
}

trials.output:
All files are in place. Here's the module structure:

**`@node/quiz-builder`** — a complete quiz builder module with these files:

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Module metadata with MSS tags (`education`, `collection`, `ask`) |
| `skills/quiz-builder/SKILL.md` | Seed skill description |
| `types.ts` | `Quiz`, `Question`, and `QuizAttempt` types |
| `store.ts` | JSON persistence to `data/quizzes.json` via Bun APIs |
| `render.ts` | Server-side HTML rendering with plaited `h()` templates |
| `index.ts` | Bun HTTP server with all routes |
| `data/quizzes.json` | Persistent quiz storage (starts empty) |

**Routes:**
- `GET /` — Quiz list with "Create Quiz" and per-quiz "Take" / "Delete" buttons
- `GET /create` — Form to build a quiz: title + dynamically addable questions (each with 4 options, radio to mark correct)
- `POST /create` — Saves quiz to `data/quizzes.json`
- `GET /take/:id` — Starts a quiz attempt, shows question 1
- `POST /take/:id/answer` — Records answer, advances to next question or shows score
- `GET /delete/:id` — Removes a quiz
- `GET /add-question?index=N` — Returns an HTML fragment for dynamic "Add Question" on the create form

**Take-quiz flow:** Questions appear one at a time with a progress bar. After the last question, a score screen shows the total (e.g. "3/5, 60%") and a per-question breakdown with correct/incorrect indicators, the user's answer, and the correct answer for any missed questions. Users can retake or return to the list.

---
name: quiz-builder
description: Create and take multiple-choice quizzes with per-question scoring
metadata:
  contentType: education
  structure: collection
  boundary: ask
  scale: "2"
---

# Quiz Builder

A module for creating and taking multiple-choice quizzes.

## Features

- **Create quizzes** with a title and any number of multiple-choice questions
- Each question has 4 answer options with one marked correct
- **Take quizzes** one question at a time with a next button
- **Score screen** shows total correct and per-question breakdown
- Quiz definitions persist as JSON in the data directory

## Usage

### Creating a Quiz
1. Navigate to the quiz builder home
2. Click "Create Quiz"
3. Enter a quiz title
4. Add questions: provide question text, four answer options, and select the correct one
5. Save the quiz

### Taking a Quiz
1. Select a quiz from the list
2. Answer each question and click "Next"
3. View your final score with a per-question breakdown showing which you got right or wrong

   183→    style: { ...styles.card, position: 'relative' },
   184→    'data-question-index': String(index),
   185→  },
   186→    h('h3', { style: styles.subheading }, `Question ${index + 1}`),
   187→    h('label', { style: styles.label }, 'Question Text'),
   188→    h('input', {
   189→      type: 'text',
   190→      name: `q_${index}_text`,
   191→      placeholder: 'Enter question...',
   192→      required: 'true',
   193→      style: styles.input,
   194→    }),
   195→    ...([0, 1, 2, 3] as const).map((optIdx) =>
   196→      h('div', { style: styles.optionRow },
   197→        h('input', {
   198→          type: 'radio',
   199→          name: `q_${index}_correct`,
   200→          value: String(optIdx),
   201→          required: 'true',
   202→          style: styles.radio,
   203→          ...(optIdx === 0 ? { checked: 'true' } : {}),
   204→        }),
   205→        h('input', {
   206→          type: 'text',
   207→          name: `q_${index}_opt_${optIdx}`,
   208→          placeholder: `Option ${optIdx + 1}`,
   209→          required: 'true',
   210→          style: { ...styles.input, marginBottom: '0', flex: '1' },
   211→        }),
   212→      ),
   213→    ),
   214→    h('p', { style: styles.muted }, 'Select the radio button next to the correct answer.'),
   215→  );
   216→
   217→export const renderTakeQuestion = (attempt: QuizAttempt) => {
   218→  const q = attempt.questions[attempt.currentQuestion];
   219→  const progress = attempt.currentQuestion / attempt.questions.length;
   220→  const isLast = attempt.currentQuestion === attempt.questions.length - 1;
   221→
   222→  return h('div', { style: styles.container },
   223→    h('h1', { style: styles.heading }, attempt.quizTitle),
   224→    h('p', { style: styles.muted },
   225→      `Question ${attempt.currentQuestion + 1} of ${attempt.questions.length}`,
   226→    ),
   227→    h('div', { style: styles.progressBar },
   228→      h('div', {
   229→        style: {
   230→          width: `${Math.round(progress * 100)}%`,
   231→          height: '100%',
   232→          backgroundColor: '#0f3460',
   233→          borderRadius: '4px',
   234→          transition: 'width 0.3s',
   235→        },
   236→      }),
   237→    ),
   238→    h('form', { method: 'POST', action: `/take/${attempt.quizId}/answer` },
   239→      h('div', { style: styles.card },
   240→        h('h2', { style: { ...styles.subheading, fontSize: '18px' } }, q.text),
   241→        ...q.options.map((opt, idx) =>
   242→          h('label', {
   243→            style: {
   244→              ...styles.optionRow,
   245→              padding: '10px 12px',
   246→              borderRadius: '6px',
   247→              cursor: 'pointer',
   248→              border: '1px solid #e0e0e0',
   249→              backgroundColor: '#fff',
   250→            },
   251→          },
   252→            h('input', {
   253→              type: 'radio',
   254→              name: 'answer',
   255→              value: String(idx),
   256→              required: 'true',
   257→              style: styles.radio,
   258→            }),
   259→            h('span', {}, opt),
   260→          ),
   261→        ),
   262→      ),
   263→      h('input', { type: 'hidden', name: 'questionIndex', value: String(attempt.currentQuestion) }),
   264→      h('button', { type: 'submit', style: styles.button },
   265→        isLast ? 'Finish Quiz' : 'Next',
   266→      ),
   267→    ),
   268→  );
   269→};
   270→
   271→export const renderScore = (attempt: QuizAttempt) => {
   272→  const total = attempt.questions.length;
   273→  const correct = attempt.questions.filter(
   274→    (q, i) => attempt.answers[i] === q.correctIndex,
   275→  ).length;
   276→  const pct = Math.round((correct / total) * 100);
   277→
   278→  return h('div', { style: styles.container },
   279→    h('h1', { style: styles.heading }, 'Quiz Results'),
   280→    h('h2', { style: { ...styles.subheading, textAlign: 'center' } }, attempt.quizTitle),
   281→    h('div', { style: styles.scoreCircle }, `${correct}/${total}`),
   282→    h('p', {
   283→      style: { textAlign: 'center', fontSize: '18px', marginBottom: '24px', color: '#555' },
   284→    }, `You scored ${pct}%`),
   285→    h('h3', { style: styles.subheading }, 'Question Breakdown'),
   286→    ...attempt.questions.map((q, i) => {
   287→      const userAnswer = attempt.answers[i];
   288→      const isCorrect = userAnswer === q.correctIndex;
   289→      return h('div', {
   290→        style: {
   291→          ...styles.card,
   292→          borderLeft: `4px solid ${isCorrect ? '#27ae60' : '#c0392b'}`,
   293→        },
   294→      },
   295→        h('p', { style: { fontWeight: '600', margin: '0 0 8px' } },
   296→          `${i + 1}. ${q.text}`,
   297→        ),
   298→        h('p', { style: isCorrect ? styles.correct : styles.incorrect },
   299→          isCorrect ? 'Correct' : 'Incorrect',
   300→        ),
   301→        h('p', { style: { margin: '4px 0', fontSize: '14px' } },
   302→          `Your answer: ${q.options[userAnswer] ?? 'No answer'}`,
   303→        ),
   304→        !isCorrect
   305→          ? h('p', { style: { ...styles.correct, fontSize: '14px', margin: '4px 0' } },
   306→              `Correct answer: ${q.options[q.correctIndex]}`,
   307→            )
   308→          : null,
   309→      );
   310→    }),
   311→    h('div', { style: { marginTop: '20px' } },
   312→      h('a', { href: `/take/${attempt.quizId}`, style: styles.button }, 'Retake Quiz'),
   313→      h('a', { href: '/', style: styles.buttonSecondary }, 'Back to Quizzes'),
   314→    ),
   315→  );
   316→};
   317→

trajectory.content: Let me verify all files are in order.

All files are in place. Here's the module structure:

**`@node/quiz-builder`** — a complete quiz builder module with these files:

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Module metadata with MSS tags (`education`, `collection`, `ask`) |
| `skills/quiz-builder/SKILL.md` | Seed skill description |
| `types.ts` | `Quiz`, `Question`, and `QuizAttempt` types |
| `store.ts` | JSON persistence to `data/quizzes.json` via Bun APIs |
| `render.ts` | Server-side HTML rendering with plaited `h()` templates |
| `index.ts` | Bun HTTP server with all routes |
| `data/quizzes.json` | Persistent quiz storage (starts empty) |

**Routes:**
- `GET /` — Quiz list with "Create Quiz" and per-quiz "Take" / "Delete" buttons
- `GET /create` — Form to build a quiz: title + dynamically addable questions (each with 4 options, radio to mark correct)
- `POST /create` — Saves quiz to `data/quizzes.json`
- `GET /take/:id` — Starts a quiz attempt, shows question 1
- `POST /take/:id/answer` — Records answer, advances to next question or shows score
- `GET /delete/:id` — Removes a quiz
- `GET /add-question?index=N` — Returns an HTML fragment for dynamic "Add Question" on the create form

**Take-quiz flow:** Questions appear one at a time with a progress bar. After the last question, a score screen shows the total (e.g. "3/5, 60%") and a per-question breakdown with correct/incorrect indicators, the user's answer, and the correct answer for any missed questions. Users can retake or return to the list.
